channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: David Szalay’s Booker Prize novel compared to Kubrick’s Barry Lyndon over plot similarities
item.description: David Szalay, the Booker Prize-winning author of the novel Flesh, has come under scrutiny after critics pointed out notable similarities between his work and Stanley Kubrick’s 1975 film Barry Lyndon. ...

item.title: LTFRB adopts online system for Service Contracting Program applications to speed up processing for PUV operators
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has announced that it will now use an online platform to process applications for the government’s Land-Based Service Contracting Progr...

item.title: Brad Treling and Jason Spezza to Manage Canada's Hockey Team at 2026 World Championship
item.description: Canada’s national hockey team will be led by former Toronto Maple Leafs general manager Brad Treling and Pittsburgh Penguins assistant GM Jason Spezza at the 2026 world hockey championship. Hockey Can...

item.title: Trump clarifies deleted image was meant to show him as a doctor, not Jesus
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump addressed questions regarding a now-deleted image posted on his Truth Social account that many interpreted as portraying him as Jesus Christ. Speaking to reporters outside ...

item.title: Dream recall and nightmares may signal early signs of Alzheimer’s in older adults
item.description: A recent study suggests that the ability to recall dreams could be an early indicator of Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers from the University of NSW’s Centre for Healthy Brain Ageing examined over 1,0...

item.title: Court Dismisses Trump's Lawsuit Against Wall Street Journal Regarding Epstein Coverage
item.description: A federal judge has dismissed former U.S. President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal and media mogul Rupert Murdoch. The lawsuit, filed by Trump, claimed that the new...

item.title: Delays at EU Borders Cause EasyJet Passengers to Miss Flights from Milan
item.description: More than 100 EasyJet passengers missed their flight from Milan's Linate Airport to Manchester after being stuck for hours in lengthy queues at border control caused by the European Union’s new Entry-...

item.title: Kejriwal Seeks Justice Sharma's Recusal in Delhi Excise Case
item.description: Arvind Kejriwal, the Delhi Chief Minister, recently caused quite a stir in the Delhi High Court when he argued his own case – not concerning the excise policy matter itself, but a plea to have Justice...

item.title: African anti-corruption authorities convene in Arusha to strengthen cooperation and curb financial crimes
item.description: Top anti-corruption officials from nine African countries are meeting in Arusha, Tanzania, for a two-day session aimed at enhancing collaboration among national bureaus to address the continent’s huge...

item.title: Managing Africa's Ocean Resources to Reduce Conflicts
item.description: Over the last few decades, Africa’s oceans have become increasingly crowded due to aquaculture, energy projects like wind and wave power, and oil and gas exploration. This expansion has threatened bot...

item.title: Emfuleni residents continue to struggle with ongoing sewage problems
item.description: Residents of Tshepong township in the Emfuleni local municipality are enduring a persistent sewage problem that has been ongoing for years. Overflowing sewers have turned streets into hazardous areas,...

item.title: Pakistan mediates US-Iran talks as ceasefire holds amid regional tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that Pakistan continues to play a central role in mediating between the United States and Iran, with efforts still underway to resolve outstanding issues follow...

item.title: Zanzibar District Commissioners Sworn In to Boost Service Delivery
item.description: On Monday, April 13, 2026, Zanzibar President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi officially swore in the new district commissioners at the State House in Unguja. The ceremony brought together top government leader...

item.title: Mexico Sees Rise in Financial Inclusion Through Contactless Payments
item.description: Mexico is experiencing a significant shift towards cashless transactions, with contactless payments playing a crucial role in expanding financial inclusion. Recent data indicates that a growing number...

item.title: Middle East Negotiations Collapse: US Threatens Action, Blockade Imminent
item.description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran have failed to produce a breakthrough, escalating tensions in the Middle East and raising the risk of renewed conflict. The ceasefire, which has been in...

item.title: Seven Military Personnel Killed by IED After Repelling Terrorist Attack in Monguno
item.description: The Nigerian Army don come out talk say one commanding officer and six of him soldiers don die after one improvised explosive device (IED) blast happen for Monguno area of Borno State. The sad inciden...

item.title: Roblox introduces age-specific accounts and safety features for children under 16
item.description: Roblox, the popular online gaming platform with over 150 million daily users, has announced major safety updates aimed at protecting children under 16. This follows recent concerns about online groomi...

item.title: Police Seek Public Assistance in Identifying Home Depot Theft Suspect in Sault Ste. Marie
item.description: The Sault Ste. Marie Police Service is appealing to the public for assistance in identifying a suspect involved in a recent theft at a local Home Depot. The incident occurred recently, but details rem...

item.title: Fitch Maintains Pakistan’s Credit Rating at 'B-' Amid Economic Challenges
item.description: So, Fitch Ratings, one of the big international agencies, has given Pakistan a bit of a thumbs up on its credit rating. They’re still saying it’s a ‘B-’ with a ‘stable outlook’. Basically, they think ...

item.title: Dispute Erupts Over Short-Term Rental Restrictions at Blyde Riverwalk Estate
item.description: Owners at Balwin Properties' Blyde Riverwalk Estate in Pretoria have voiced strong opposition to a ban on short-term letting (STL) implemented by the developer, claiming the actions are unlawful, unet...
